get.description:
Get public keys for JWT token validation (RFC 7517).

Input: ?provider=internal|google|all, ?org_id=, ?refresh=true
Auth: None required (public endpoint).

USE FOR: Resource servers validating JWT tokens.

RETURNS: {keys:[{kty, kid, use, alg, n, e}], metadata:{...}}

FILTERS:
- No params: Internal keys only
- provider=all: All provider keys (deprecated, use org_id)
- org_id=X: Organization-specific keys (recommended)

CACHED: 1 hour. Use refresh=true for immediate updates.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Org-specific keys" -> GET /organizations/{org_id}/.well-known/jwks.json
- "OpenID discovery" -> GET /.well-known/openid-configuration
- "JWKS health" -> GET /.well-known/jwks.json/health

parameters.name: provider

schema.description: Filter by provider (internal, google, microsoft, all)
schema.title: Provider

parameters.description: Filter by provider (internal, google, microsoft, all)

get.description:
Real-time JWKS health status (not cached).

Input: None
Auth: None required (public endpoint).

USE FOR: Debugging token validation failures, monitoring key rotation.

RETURNS: {status, jwks_enabled, active_keys, total_keys,
          oldest_key_age_days, next_rotation, warnings:[]}

STATUS VALUES: healthy, warning, disabled, error

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Get public keys" -> GET /.well-known/jwks.json
- "Admin key rotation" -> GET /admin/jwks/rotation-status
- "Service health" -> GET /health

List API keys - your own keys, or all org keys if admin.

Input: None
Auth: Bearer token or API key required.

VISIBILITY:
- Regular users: see only their own keys
- Org admins: see all keys in their organization
- Super admins: see keys across their organizations

RETURNS: [{key_id, name, permissions[], created_at, expires_at, last_used}]
NOTE: Token value is NOT returned (only shown on creation).

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Create new key" -> POST /api-keys/
- "Get specific key" -> GET /api-keys/{key_id}
- "Revoke a key" -> DELETE /api-keys/{key_id}

schema.title: Response Get Api Keys Api Keys  Get

post.description:
Create API key for service-to-service auth.

Input: {name, permissions?, rate_limit?, expires_at?, metadata?}
Auth: Bearer token required. Key is created for current user's org.

SCENARIOS:
1. Basic key: {name:"My Service"} -> key with no specific permissions
2. Scoped key: {name, permissions:["api.read","api.write"]} -> limited scope
3. Rate-limited: {name, rate_limit:1000} -> 1000 req/hour limit
4. Expiring key: {name, expires_at:"2025-12-31T00:00:00Z"} -> auto-expires

RETURNS: {key_id, token} - SAVE THE TOKEN, it's only shown once!

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Validate an API key" -> POST /auth/validate-api-key {api_key}
- "List my API keys" -> GET /api-keys/
- "Revoke a key" -> DELETE /api-keys/{key_id}
- "Use key for auth" -> Header: X-API-Key: {token}

delete.description:
Permanently revoke an API key.

Input: key_id in path
Auth: Bearer token or API key required (must own key or be admin).

WARNING: This is permanent. The key will immediately stop working.
Any services using this key will lose access.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Disable temporarily" -> PUT /api-keys/{key_id} {is_active:false}
- "Create replacement key" -> POST /api-keys/
- "List remaining keys" -> GET /api-keys/

post.description: Check if user has specific permission

Authenticate existing user and get access tokens.

Input: {email, password, org_id?, provider_type?}
Returns: {access_token, refresh_token, user: {id, email, org_id}}

SCENARIOS:
1. Single org user: {email, password} -> logs into default/personal org
2. Multi-org user: {email, password, org_id} -> logs into specific org
3. OAuth provider: {email, password, provider_type:"google"} -> provider auth

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "I don't have an account" -> POST /auth/register
- "I forgot my password" -> POST /users/request-password-reset
- "I need to refresh my token" -> POST /token/refresh
- "Which orgs can I access?" -> GET /users/me/organizations (after login)

get.description: Get current authenticated user info

post.description:
Get new access token using refresh token.

Input: {refresh_token}
Auth: None required (refresh_token authenticates the request).

USE WHEN: Access token expired (15min lifetime) but refresh token still valid.
RETURNS: New {access_token, refresh_token, user, expires_in}

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Token expired, need to login again" -> POST /auth/login
- "Check if my token is still valid" -> POST /auth/validate-token
- "Logout completely" -> POST /auth/logout

Register new user with internal authentication.

Input: {email, password, name, org_id?, invitation_code?}
Password: min 8 chars, uppercase, lowercase, digit, special. See GET /status

REGISTRATION FLOWS:
1. Individual (no org): {email, password, name} -> creates personal workspace
2. Join org via invite: {email, password, name, org_id, invitation_code} -> joins existing org
3. Join org (open): {email, password, name, org_id} -> if org allows open registration

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "I want to create an account" -> POST /auth/register (this endpoint)
- "I'm an admin, invite someone" -> POST /organizations/{org_id}/invite
- "I have an account, need to login" -> POST /auth/login
- "I want to create an organization" -> POST /organizations/ (requires auth)
- "List my organizations" -> GET /users/me/organizations (requires auth)

Switch to a different organization context.

Input: {org_id}
Auth: Bearer token required. Must be member of target org.

USE WHEN: User belongs to multiple orgs and wants to change active context.
RETURNS: New {access_token, refresh_token} with org_id embedded.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "List my organizations" -> GET /users/me/organizations
- "Check which org I'm in" -> GET /users/me (see active_org_id)
- "Join a new organization" -> POST /auth/register {org_id, invitation_code}
- "Create new organization" -> POST /organizations/

Validate API key - for microservices to verify service-to-service auth.

Input: {api_key, required_permissions?}
Auth: None required (this IS the validation endpoint).

SCENARIOS:
1. Basic check: {api_key} -> returns {valid, user_id, org_id, permissions}
2. Permission check: {api_key, required_permissions:["api.read"]} -> also checks perms

RETURNS: {valid:bool, user_id, org_id, permissions[], reason?}

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Validate user JWT instead" -> POST /auth/validate-token
- "Create new API key" -> POST /api-keys/
- "List my API keys" -> GET /api-keys/

Validate JWT token - for microservices to verify user tokens.

Input: {token, required_permissions?, resource_type?, resource_id?}
Auth: None required (this IS the validation endpoint).

SCENARIOS:
1. Basic check: {token} -> returns {valid, user_id, org_id, permissions}
2. Permission check: {token, required_permissions:["api.read"]} -> also checks perms
3. Resource check: {token, resource_type:"file", resource_id:"123"} -> resource-level

RETURNS: {valid:bool, user_id, org_id, permissions[], reason?}

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Validate API key instead" -> POST /auth/validate-api-key
- "Get new token" -> POST /auth/refresh or POST /auth/login
- "Check specific permission" -> POST /permissions/check

get.description:
Quick service health check for load balancers and monitoring.

Input: None
Auth: None required (public endpoint).

RETURNS: {status:"healthy|degraded|unhealthy", dependencies:{}, metrics:{}}

USE FOR: Kubernetes probes, load balancer health checks, basic monitoring.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Detailed config info" -> GET /status
- "JWT key health" -> GET /health/jwks
- "API documentation" -> GET /docs

Check JWT signing key health and availability.

Input: None
Auth: None required (public endpoint).

RETURNS: {status, active_key_id, key_count, last_rotation}

USE FOR: Debugging token validation failures, monitoring key rotation.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Get public keys" -> GET /.well-known/jwks.json
- "Emergency key recovery" -> POST /health/jwks/recover (admin only)
- "Basic health" -> GET /health

schema.title: Response Jwks Health Health Jwks Get

post.description:
EMERGENCY: Recover JWT signing capability when no active key exists.

Input: None
Auth: NONE - Intentionally unprotected for bootstrap/recovery scenarios.
      (If signing keys are broken, you can't authenticate to fix them.)

PRODUCTION: Protect via network-level controls (internal-only access).

USE ONLY WHEN: Service cannot generate tokens due to missing/corrupted keys.

BEHAVIOR:
1. If active key exists -> Returns "no_action_needed" (safe, no changes)
2. If keys exist but none active -> Activates newest key
3. If no keys exist -> Generates and activates new key

RETURNS: {status, message, active_key_id}

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Normal key rotation (requires auth)" -> POST /admin/jwks/rotate
- "JWKS health check" -> GET /health/jwks
- "Key status" -> GET /admin/jwks/rotation-status

schema.title: Response Jwks Recover Health Jwks Recover Post

post.description:
Create a new organization. Requires authentication.

Input: {name, slug?, billing_type?, domain?, parent_id?}
Auth: Bearer token required. Creator becomes org admin.

SCENARIOS:
1. New team org: {name:"My Team"} -> creates org, you become admin
2. Child org: {name, parent_id} -> creates sub-org under parent
3. With billing: {name, billing_type:"prepaid"} -> sets billing tier

QUOTA LIMITS:
- Free tier: 1 org per user
- Paid tiers: Higher limits (see GET /quotas/tiers)
- Returns 429 if quota exceeded

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "I want a personal account" -> POST /auth/register (creates personal workspace)
- "Invite someone to my org" -> POST /organizations/{org_id}/invite
- "List my organizations" -> GET /users/me/organizations
- "Check my quota" -> GET /quotas/my-usage

get.description: Get organization details

Get organization-specific public keys for token validation.

Input: org_id in path, ?refresh=true
Auth: None required (public endpoint).

USE FOR: Validating tokens in multi-tenant/auth mesh architectures.

RETURNS: {keys:[...], metadata:{org_id, providers:[...]}}

INCLUDES:
- Organization's internal signing keys
- Keys from all OAuth providers configured for this org

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Global JWKS" -> GET /.well-known/jwks.json
- "OpenID discovery" -> GET /.well-known/openid-configuration
- "Org's configured providers" -> GET /providers/?org_id={org_id}
Get organization hierarchy with teams and users
List all invitations for an organization (admin only)

post.description:
Invite user to join organization by email.

Input: {email, role, team_id?, permissions?, message?, expires_at?}
Auth: Bearer token with users.invite permission required.

SCENARIOS:
1. Basic invite: {email, role:"member"} -> sends invite email
2. Team invite: {email, role, team_id} -> invites to specific team
3. Admin invite: {email, role:"admin"} -> invites as org admin
4. Custom perms: {email, role, permissions:["api.read"]} -> grants specific perms

INVITE FLOW:
- Invite created -> email sent -> user registers with invite code -> joins org

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "User wants to join my org" -> User calls POST /auth/register {org_id, invitation_code}
- "List pending invites" -> GET /organizations/{org_id}/invitations
- "Create the org first" -> POST /organizations/
- "Add existing user directly" -> POST /organizations/{org_id}/members

get.description: Get all active sessions in the organization (admin only)

delete.description:
Force logout ALL users in the organization (emergency use).

Input: org_id in path
Auth: Bearer token with org.admin permission required.

WARNING: All users will be logged out immediately! Use for security incidents.
Users must re-authenticate to regain access.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Logout specific user" -> DELETE /organizations/{org_id}/users/{user_id}/sessions
- "Just logout myself" -> POST /auth/logout
- "Revoke specific token" -> POST /auth/revoke

get.description:
List all teams in an organization.

Input: org_id in path
Auth: Bearer token with teams.read permission required.

RETURNS: [{id, name, slug, description, permissions[], member_count}]

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Create new team" -> POST /organizations/{org_id}/teams
- "Get team details" -> GET /teams/{team_id}
- "List team members" -> GET /teams/{team_id}/members
- "List org members (not teams)" -> GET /organizations/{org_id}/users

schema.title: Response Get Organization Teams Organizations  Org Id  Teams Get

delete.description:
Force logout a specific user from the organization.

Input: org_id, user_id in path
Auth: Bearer token with org.admin permission required.

USE FOR: Compromised accounts, employee termination, security incidents.
User must re-authenticate to regain access.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Logout all org users" -> DELETE /organizations/{org_id}/sessions
- "Remove user from org" -> DELETE /organizations/{org_id}/users/{user_id}
- "Deactivate user account" -> PUT /users/{user_id} {status:"inactive"}

post.description:
Grant a permission to a user within an organization.

Input: ?user_id={user_id}&org_id={org_id}&permission={permission}
Auth: Bearer token with users.write permission required.

SCENARIOS:
1. Grant role perm: ?permission=api.read -> grants specific permission
2. Grant wildcard: ?permission=api.* -> grants all api.* permissions
3. Grant admin: ?permission=org.admin -> makes user org admin

COMMON PERMISSIONS: api.read, api.write, users.read, users.write,
org.admin, billing.read, tools.use. See GET /permissions/roles for role bundles.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Check if user has permission" -> POST /permissions/check {user_id, permission}
- "List user's permissions" -> GET /permissions/user/{user_id}?org_id=
- "Remove permission" -> POST /permissions/revoke ?user_id&org_id&permission
- "See available roles" -> GET /permissions/roles

Register custom permissions for your service.

Input: {service, description, actions:[], resources:[], metadata?}
Auth: Bearer token or API key with permissions.register permission required.

EXAMPLE: Register "myapp" service with actions ["read", "write"]
         Creates permissions: myapp.read, myapp.write

RETURNS: {valid, service, permissions_registered:[]}

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "See registered services" -> GET /permissions/registry/services
- "Grant registered permission" -> POST /permissions/grant
- "Validate permission string" -> POST /permissions/registry/validate

get.description:
List all registered services and their permission schemas.

Input: None
Auth: None required (public discovery endpoint).

RETURNS: {services:[{name, actions, resources}]}

USE FOR: Discovering what permissions exist before granting.
PERMISSION FORMAT: "service.action" (e.g., "users.read", "api.write")

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Get all valid permissions" -> GET /permissions/registry/valid-permissions
- "Validate a permission" -> POST /permissions/registry/validate
- "Grant a permission" -> POST /permissions/grant

Get permission registry statistics.

Input: None
Auth: None required (public endpoint).

RETURNS: {total_services, total_permissions, services_list}

USE FOR: Admin dashboards, understanding permission system scope.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "List all services" -> GET /permissions/registry/services
- "List all permissions" -> GET /permissions/registry/valid-permissions

List all valid permission strings, optionally filtered by service.

Input: ?service= (optional filter)
Auth: None required (public discovery endpoint).

Without filter: Returns ALL permissions from all registered services.
With filter: Returns only permissions for specified service.

RETURNS: {permissions:["service.action", ...], service}

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "See services" -> GET /permissions/registry/services
- "Validate specific permission" -> POST /permissions/registry/validate
- "Grant a permission" -> POST /permissions/grant

post.description:
Validate a permission string format and existence.

Input: ?permission=service.action
Auth: None required (public endpoint).

Checks: Format valid (service.action), service registered, action exists.

RETURNS: {valid:bool, reason?, service?, action?}

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "List valid permissions" -> GET /permissions/registry/valid-permissions
- "Grant if valid" -> POST /permissions/grant

get.description:
Get list of predefined roles and their bundled permissions.

Input: None
Auth: None required (public discovery endpoint).

AVAILABLE ROLES: admin, developer, viewer, billing_admin

RETURNS: {roles:{role_name:{name, description, permissions:[]}}}

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Grant specific permission" -> POST /permissions/grant
- "Assign role to user" -> PUT /organizations/{org_id}/members/{user_id}
- "See all possible permissions" -> GET /permissions/registry/valid-permissions

List all permissions granted to a user.

Input: user_id (path), ?org_id= (optional filter)
Auth: Bearer token + users.read permission required.

Without org_id: Returns all permissions across all orgs.
With org_id: Returns permissions only within that org.

RETURNS: {user_id, org_id, permissions:[]}

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Check specific permission" -> POST /permissions/check
- "Grant a permission" -> POST /permissions/grant
- "Revoke a permission" -> POST /permissions/revoke

Check if creating a resource would exceed quota.

Input: resource_type in path, ?org_id= optional
Auth: Bearer token required.

RESOURCE TYPES: organizations_per_user, api_keys_per_user, teams_per_org, etc.

RETURNS: {allowed:bool, message, current_usage, limit, remaining}

USE BEFORE: Creating orgs, API keys, teams to avoid quota errors.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "See all my usage" -> GET /quotas/my-usage
- "See tier limits" -> GET /quotas/tiers
- "Create organization" -> POST /organizations/ (returns 429 if quota exceeded)

schema.title: Response Check Quota Quotas Check  Resource Type  Get

get.description:
Get your current quota usage and limits.

Input: None
Auth: Bearer token required.

RETURNS: {tier, usage:{orgs, api_keys, ...}, limits:{...}, percentages:{...}}

USE FOR: Checking usage before creating resources, displaying quota UI.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Check if I can create X" -> GET /quotas/check/{resource_type}
- "See all tier limits" -> GET /quotas/tiers
- "Upgrade my plan" -> See upgrade_url in response

schema.title: Response Get My Quota Usage Quotas My Usage Get

get.description:
List all quota tiers and their limits.

Input: None
Auth: None required (public endpoint).

RETURNS: {tiers:{free:{limits}, pro:{limits}, ...}, upgrade_url}

USE FOR: Displaying pricing/limits comparison, planning capacity.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Check my current tier" -> GET /quotas/my-usage (see tier field)
- "Upgrade my plan" -> Visit upgrade_url
- "Check specific quota" -> GET /quotas/check/{resource_type}

schema.title: Response Get Quota Tiers Quotas Tiers Get

get.description:
Get current authenticated user's profile.

Input: None
Auth: Bearer token required.

RETURNS: {id, email, name, status, organizations[], permissions[], ...}

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Update my profile" -> PUT /users/me
- "List my organizations" -> GET /users/me/organizations
- "Change my password" -> POST /users/me/change-password
- "Switch organization context" -> POST /auth/switch-organization

put.description:
Update current user's profile fields.

Input: {name?, phone?, avatar_url?, timezone?, language?, metadata?}
Auth: Bearer token required.

NOTE: Only provided fields are updated (partial update). Email cannot be changed here.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Get my current profile" -> GET /users/me
- "Change my password" -> POST /users/me/change-password
- "Change my email" -> POST /users/me/change-email (requires verification)

post.description:
Change current user's password (requires current password).

Input: {current_password, new_password}
Auth: Bearer token required.
Note: new_password must meet policy. See GET /status for password_policy.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Forgot my password" -> POST /auth/request-password-reset {email}
- "Update my profile" -> PUT /users/me
- "Get my profile" -> GET /users/me

get.description:
List all organizations the current user belongs to.

Input: None
Auth: Bearer token required.

RETURNS: [{id, name, type, role, is_personal, is_default, permissions[]}]

USE FOR: Building org switcher UI, checking multi-org membership.

RELATED ENDPOINTS BY INTENT:
- "Switch to different org" -> POST /auth/switch-organization {org_id}
- "Leave an organization" -> DELETE /organizations/{org_id}/members/me
- "Create new organization" -> POST /organizations/
- "Get my profile" -> GET /users/me

schema.title: Response Get My Organizations Users Me Organizations Get
